title: Mapped Type Transformer

statement.markdown:
# Mapped Type Transformer
You are given a type `P` and a set of types `A` and you need to create a new type `U` that takes a `P` and returns an object with `A` types.
### Requirements
1. Create a mapped type transformer called `Transform<U>` that takes a type `P` and returns an object with types `A`.
2. The type `U` should have the same shape as the original `P` type.
3. You should use the mapped type `P[A]` to create a new property in `U`.
4. You should use the `keyof P` type to get the possible keys of the object `P`.

constraints: Preserve the shape of the original type.
Use keyof P to get possible keys of P.

gradingCriteria.description: Mapped type transformer creates a new type with shape of the original type
The type transformer takes a type P and returns an object with types A
The mapped type 'P[A]' is used to create a new property in the type transformer
The 'keyof P' type is used to get the possible keys of the object P
